Glossary








Term
Definition




linear



non-linear



diffeomorphism
A continuously differentiable map \(f: N \to M\) between two manifolds, such that \(f^{-1}: M \to N\) is also differentiable. If \(f\) is \(r\)-times differentiable it is called a \(C^r\)-diffeomorphism.


hyper surfaces
Coming from the analogy of a surface in 3D space is a 2D object. An geometric object that lives in a \(n\)–dimensional space, though itself is a \(n-1\)–dimensinal object.


isolating integral



non isolating integral



ergodic



integrable



Hamiltonian
The total energy function of a system, encoding kinetic and potential energy. ai-generated!


Lyapunov exponent
Average rate of divergence of nearby trajectories. Positive values indicate chaos. ai-generated!


Poincaré section
Intersections of a trajectory with a chosen hyperplane in phase space. ai-generated!


phase space
The space of all possible states, with axes for each position and momentum coordinate. ai-generated!


Hénon-Heiles potential
Two-dimensional potential modelling chaotic motion: V = ½(x²+y²) + x²y − ⅓y³. ai-generated!

Latest: 28 May, 2026

To do!

original paper by Henon and Heiles Henon and Heiles (1964)
[ ]

yesterdays todo’s:*

investigation of ‘How to implement?’ and ‘How to visiualize?’
simple implementation of various and visualization




checking in
I feel that I should read about implementing the Hénon & Heiles. I should also read their original paper.
Whilst working on Hénon & Heiles, I realize I need to get an in depth understanding of the Lagrange and Hamiltonian formalism!


Notes from Hénon & Heiles

on isolating and non isolating integrals A CLASSIFICATION OF THE INTEGRALS OF MOTION by George Contopoulos







Citation Henon and Heiles (1964): On the argument of 2 or 3 non isolating integrals.






I did not understand much of section 2
a second read and note shed some light, here is an interesting paragraph describing what ergodic mean







Citation Henon and Heiles (1964): On finding the second Integral. Leading to an understanding of what it means to be ergodic.






a question they seem to be answering is how to find a proof of the existence of an non isolating integral
they speak of two integration schemes:

Adams
Runge-Kutta

Their analysis introduces terms as islands and chain of islands, dimension of islands, dense and need to be understood when I study this model







Citation Henon and Heiles (1964): Statements and potential open questions.






“but the dimensions are so small that finding them is difficult” – is how the term dimensions gets used again

To do!

start github
initialize Julia environment for the project
investigation of ‘How to implement?’ and ‘How to visiualize?’
simple implementation of various and visualization




Interests:

universality -> quantum chaos A Field Theory Approach to Universality in Quantum Chaos

random matrix theory?

phase transitions as models for non-linear systems
how non-linear systems develop as a function of degrees of freedom




Issues:

low understanding of basic concepts
I want to learn by doing, so that I come across basic concepts and problems
how to find a model to start?




talk with Benoit:

Hénon Heilses Hamiltonian -> classical chaos (connection to Toda potential, integrable)

\[V(x,y)=1/2(x^2+y^2)+x^2y - 1/3 y^3\]

Heno Heiles model, by Toda potential -> 3D; resulting in Heno Heiles 3D?
obtaining higher order DOF by taking integral system, pert
find the models in the books
computational methods (pioncare cross section)

title: 27 May, 2026
text:
Also available as a slide deck.




Yesterdays To do!

start github
initialize Julia environment for the project
investigation of ‘How to implement?’ and ‘How to visiualize?’
simple implementation of various and visualization




getting started: Hénon Heils Model
Comments for the day:
I first worked on some further workflow questions. Including a bash script for adding entries from the ‘glossary.json’ to the ‘glossary.qmd’ file and to the ’_quarto.qmd’ file, for latex macros or to initiate the day-log by the use of templates.
I use the ai-tool provided by claude to help generate the bash-scripts for workflow and to communicate about the structure of my workspace. I have general idea from previous projects how I want to structure my workspace - though I do not have the knowledge in coding in ‘bash’ or ‘yml’ etc…
So I use ai to set up my folder structure and to prepare the workflow. I also need to keep track of my ai usage for transparency.


suggested structure

derivation
considering derivation
numerical implementation




observations

original paper by Henon and Heiles Henon and Heiles (1964)

I came across, need to undertand: 6-D phase space -> 5 independant conservative integrals of the motion; seemingly, sure but I want a rigorous understanding
results in 5 hyper surfaces, their intersections define the trajectories




literature by Strogatz (1994) under chapter 12 strange attractors
wikipedia

‘orbit diagram’ or ‘bifracation diagram’
is the Héno Map the same as the Hénon–Heiles model? I dont think so!






Lost traveler thoughts

connections of strange attractor to Cantor Set Arnold (1989) p. 430-431;
gets me to think about how ternary number representation (base 3) is a useful way to analyze the Cantor set







How the manningfold gets created.







The relation to the cantor set.





…





References

Arnold, Vladimir Igorevich. 1989. Mathematical Methods of Classical Mechanics. 2nd ed. Translated by K. Vogtmann and A. Weinstein. Vol. 60. Graduate Texts in Mathematics. Springer-Verlag.


Henon, Michel, and Carl Heiles. 1964. “The Applicability of the Third Integral of Motion: Some Numerical Experiments.” The Astronomical Journal 69 (February): 73. https://doi.org/10.1086/109234.


Strogatz, Steven H. 1994. Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos. Addison-Wesley.
